title: structured-document-text
description: Structured document representation for Zotero. Defines a semantic content tree of block nodes (paragraphs, headings, lists, tables, images, math, code, captions, notes, blockquotes) with inline text styling, cross-references, source anchors, page geometry, and a document outline. Supports PDF, EPUB, and snapshot sources.

version.description: Processor output version. Exact matches are used for cache freshness.

characterCount.description: Total character count of extracted text content (sum over all text nodes).

source.description: Source document identity and source-specific properties.

hash.description: MD5 hash of the source file (32-char hex string) used to detect changes.

fileSize.description: Size of the original source file in bytes.

properties.description: Source-specific document properties. For PDF: PDFFormatVersion, Title, Author, Subject, Keywords, Creator, Producer, CreationDate, ModDate, Language, and any custom properties. For EPUB: dc:title, dc:creator, dc:language, etc. For snapshot: title, url, charset. Shape is open-ended.

catalog.description: Document-level catalog structures that point into content.

pages.description: Per-page info. For PDF: physical pages. For EPUB: page mappings (physical page numbers or EPUB locations).

pageMappingType.description: For EPUB: whether pages represent physical page numbers from the source or synthetic EPUB locations.

domMap.description: For snapshots: skeleton of the source DOM - every element containing extracted text, plus its ancestors - with body text offsets. Lets consumers resolve and generate CssSelector/TextPositionSelector positions rooted at arbitrary elements without access to the source DOM.

content.description: Top-level block nodes (headings, paragraphs, lists, tables, etc.).

RefsArray.description: Referenced target nodes. Array because one mention can resolve to multiple targets (e.g. "[2-5]" -> multiple bibliography entries, or ambiguous author-name citations).

BackRefsArray.description: Nodes that reference this one (inverse of refs).

PartRef.description: Reference to the adjacent physical block that is part of the same logical block.

FlowClass.description: Semantic flow classification assigned by layout extraction on top-level content blocks. Missing means body; new producers omit body.

PageRect.description: Block bounding box: [pageIndex, x1, y1, x2, y2] (page coordinates).

RefPath.description: Path to a node in the content tree as an array of child indices. E.g. [2, 0, 1] -> content[2].content[0].content[1].

Anchor.description: Link from this node back to the source document. PDF uses PdfAnchor (pageRects/textMap). EPUB and snapshot use DomAnchor: a selectorMap field that compactly encodes source locations (blocks, EPUB text nodes), or a body text-stream offset (snapshot text nodes, resolved against catalog.domMap). Use the dom/epub decode and dom/snapshot dommap modules to expand and resolve.

DomAnchor.description: Compact anchor for EPUB and snapshot blocks and text nodes. For blocks, selectorMap is an absolute location: EPUB CFI path (starts with '/') or snapshot CSS selector. For EPUB text nodes, selectorMap is a CFI suffix relative to the parent block; multi-entry selectorMaps (from merged adjacent text nodes) are newline-separated 'charLen selectorMap' entries starting with a digit. Snapshot text nodes store `stream` instead: the raw character offset of the DOM text node's start within the body text stream, located via catalog.domMap.

selectorMap.description: Compact location string. Blocks: absolute (EPUB CFI path starting with '/', or snapshot CSS selector). EPUB text nodes: CFI suffix relative to the parent block.

stream.description: Snapshot text nodes: offset of the DOM text node's first character within the body text stream (raw original characters, counted like a NodeIterator text walk over <body>). Element positions in the same stream are recorded in catalog.domMap.

deltaMap.description: Position map between the node's text and the original DOM text it was extracted from (covers whitespace collapsing and NFC normalization). Space-separated 'nfcPos delta' pairs recorded where delta = nfcIdx - origIdx changes. Absent when all positions are 1:1. Decode: origPos = nfcPos - getDelta(deltaMap, nfcPos).

DomMapNode.description: One element in the snapshot DOM skeleton (catalog.domMap). `index` is the element's 0-based position among its parent's element children in the full source DOM (not just skeleton nodes). `textStart`/`textLength` locate the element's subtree text within the body text stream. `flags` holds sibling-position bits used for selector generation and matching: 1 = first of its tag, 2 = last of its tag, 4 = last element child.

index.description: 0-based index among the parent's element children.

prevId.description: id of the previous element sibling, for matching '#id + tag' selectors.

textStart.description: Body text-stream offset at element entry.

textLength.description: Raw character length of the element's subtree text.

children.description: Skeleton children, in document order. Omitted when empty.

PdfAnchor.description: PDF source anchor with page geometry and/or text layout map. `textMap` validation is intentionally permissive (string-only); consumers should parse and validate decoded run structure at runtime.

textMap.description: Packed text layout encoded as a JSON string. Decoded value is an array of runs; each run is `[header, pageIndex, minX, minY, maxX, maxY, ...widths]`. Header bits: bit 0 = trailing soft hyphen marker, bits 1-2 = axis direction, bit 3 = right-to-left flag. Width entries are either numeric widths or `[delta, width]` pairs.

FragmentSelector.description: W3C Web Annotation Data Model FragmentSelector. Used for EPUB CFI anchors.

TextPositionSelector.description: W3C Web Annotation Data Model TextPositionSelector. Identifies a text range by character offsets within a parent element.

CssSelector.description: W3C Web Annotation Data Model CssSelector. Identifies an element via a CSS selector, optionally refined by a TextPositionSelector for sub-element targeting.

value.description: CSS selector string targeting a unique element relative to body.

refinedBy.description: Optional TextPositionSelector refinement for sub-element text ranges.

Target.description: Target location in the source or an external URL.

TextNode.description: Inline text node with optional styling and references.

text.description: Plain text, including whitespace and newlines as-is.

reference.description: True if this block is a reference entry (e.g. bibliography item).

MathNode.description: Math block represented as inline text nodes.

content.description: Inline content of the math block.

ImageNode.description: Image block with optional inline text (alt text, labels).

content.description: Inline text associated with the image.

BlockquoteNode.description: Blockquote block that contains other blocks.

ListItemNode.description: Single list item: contains either inline text (simple items) or block children (items with nested structure).

type.description: Block type discriminator for list items.

content.description: Either inline text (TextNode[]) for simple items, or block children (ContentBlockNode[]) for items with nested structure.

reference.description: True if this block is a reference entry (e.g. bibliography item).

ordered.description: True for ordered lists, false for unordered. Defaults to false if omitted.

startIndex.description: Start index for ordered lists (defaults to 1).

CaptionNode.description: Shared caption block (for tables, figures, images, groups, etc.).

refs.description: Associated content block(s) that this caption describes (e.g. an image, table, math, or preformatted block). Caption refs indicate a 'belongs-to' relationship rather than a citation.

NoteNode.description: Note block used across the document (including table-local notes).

content.description: Inline content of the note body.

refs.description: Associated content block(s) that this note belongs to (e.g. a table, image, or math block). Note refs indicate a 'belongs-to' relationship rather than a citation.

reference.description: True if this block is a reference entry (e.g. bibliography item).

TableCellNode.description: Table cell container (header or data cell).

type.description: Block type discriminator for table cells.

header.description: If true, this is a header cell (<th>); otherwise a data cell (<td>).

colspan.description: Horizontal span of this cell. Default is 1 if omitted.

rowspan.description: Vertical span of this cell. Default is 1 if omitted.

content.description: Block content of the cell (paragraphs, lists, nested tables, etc.). Inline text must live inside those blocks.

type.description: Block type discriminator for table rows.

content.description: Cells in this row. No raw text directly under a row.
Either inline text (TextNode[]) for simple/flat tables, or structured rows (TableRowNode[]) for fully parsed tables.
Inline content of the preformatted block.

ContentBlockNode.description: Block nodes valid as document content, inside containers, and at top level. Excludes structural-only children (ListItemNode, TableRowNode, TableCellNode) that only appear inside their parent containers.

BlockNode.description: Discriminated union of all block-level node types, including structural children.

ref.description: Path to the corresponding heading or block in the content tree.

PageContentBoundary.description: Boundary position in document content. A top-level value equal to content.length is valid and means document end; deeper paths may include a terminal text offset.

PageContentRange.description: Half-open logical content range on a page: [startBoundary, endBoundary). Empty pages use equal boundaries.

PageInfo.description: Per-page metadata for the source document.

injected.description: True if the page was injected by the publisher.

viewRect.description: Effective PDF page view box in default user-space coordinates, before page rotation and UserUnit scaling: [x1, y1, x2, y2]. When CropBox and MediaBox differ and have a non-empty intersection, this is their intersection; otherwise it is MediaBox. Values can be negative when the effective box has a non-zero origin (e.g. [-50, -50, 562, 742]).

rotation.description: Effective intrinsic clockwise PDF page rotation, in degrees. In schema 1.1.0 and later, producers omit this property when 0; in earlier schemas, absence does not imply 0.

userUnit.description: Effective PDF UserUnit multiplier; one default user-space unit equals userUnit / 72 inch. In schema 1.1.0 and later, producers omit this property when 1; in earlier schemas, absence does not imply 1.

textSource.description: Source of text on this page.

extractionDegraded.description: True if this page's SDT extraction used a degraded path or should be treated as lower confidence due to text, layout, or structure extraction issues.

contentRange.description: Half-open content boundary range covered by this page. Empty pages use equal boundaries.
